prompt:
You are resuming this writing project through the `start-work` helper packet.
Live docs outrank sidecar and checkpoint evidence whenever they disagree.
Do not mutate canon or current-doc files, and do not perform silent writes.
Use this exact command first: `writing-sidecar routine "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --name start-work --verify advisory --sync never`
If you later want the sidecar-safe persisted version, use: `writing-sidecar routine "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --name start-work --verify advisory --sync never --write`
Open the listed live docs first, then move into the phase-local session command once re-entry is stable.
Open these files first:
- <PROJECT_ROOT>\_story_bible\05_Current_Notes.md
- <PROJECT_ROOT>\_story_bible\05_Current_Chapter_Notes.md
Continuity findings worth caring about now:
- [WARN] No handoff artifact exists yet: There is no brainstorm/handoff artifact capturing the carry-forward session state.
- [WARN] Artifact carry-forward lines are missing from the active live docs: Recent checkpoint/handoff/audit/discarded artifacts mention carry-forward material that is not clearly visible in the active live docs.
Next phase-local command after this packet: `writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task braindump --write`
Use the exact commands supplied by this helper packet instead of inventing new workflow steps.
expected_outputs: A clean re-entry packet with the exact files to open first.
The next phase-local session command once startup is complete.
A separate checkpoint write command if you want to persist the restart state.
recommended_actions: Use the startup bundle for re-entry, then move straight into the live phase packet instead of juggling commands manually.
Persist the startup checkpoint once real work begins so the next session does not have to reconstruct context from scratch.
Write a handoff artifact before the next session transition or closeout.
Move the still-active carry-forward threads or guardrails into the live current docs so the next session does not rely on artifacts alone.
Apply the explicit fact ops through a write-capable command when the preview looks right.
Open the suggested loadout before continuing.
Continue with writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task braindump --write when you move into the next real task.
Use lower-level search, context, or recap only when the session packet is too broad or too thin.
Run writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task braindump --write to preserve the current phase state.
Move to writing-sidecar session "<PROJECT_ROOT>" --task scripting once this phase is actually stable.
Keep live docs authoritative and use sidecar evidence only to preserve process memory and rejected options.
